sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.3 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Is it true that if$G$is a finite group with all conjugacy classes of distinct sizes, then$G { S}_3$? This is true if$G$is solvable (J.\,Zhang, J.\,Algebra, 170 (1994), 608--624); it is also known that$F(G)$is nontrivial (Z.\,Arad, M.\,Muzychuk, A.\,Oliver, J.~Algebra, 280 (2004), 537--576). { Z.\,Arad}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
